question: Will Brad T. Peters win the 2026 Oregon Governor Republican primary election?
description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the Republican Primary for Governor of Oregon, scheduled to take place on May 19, 2026. Resolution will be based on the overall winner of the primary, including any potential second round or run-off.

If no 2026 Oregon Gubernatorial Republican Primary takes place, this market will resolve to “Other.”

The resolution source for this market will be the first official announcement of the results from the Oregon Republican Party; however, an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting may suffice.

events.title: Oregon Governor Republican Primary Winner
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the Democratic Primary for Governor of Oregon, scheduled to take place on May 19, 2026. Resolution will be based on the overall winner of the primary, including any potential second round or run-off.

If no 2026 Oregon Gubernatorial Democratic Primary takes place, this market will resolve to “Other.”

The resolution source for this market will be the first official announcement of the results from the Oregon Democratic Party; however, an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting may suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on the Oregon Governor Republican Primary reflects a tight race, with Christine Drazan (36.5%) edging Ed Diehl (34.5%) amid recent polls showing them within striking distance for the 2026 GOP nomination. Drazan's 2022 near-upset as nominee bolsters her base, while Diehl's self-funding surge—over $1 million raised—and outsider appeal erode her lead, splitting support with Chris Dudley's 23% from prior name recognition. Low turnout expectations and fragmented field keep probabilities compressed; separation could emerge from January FEC reports, a key debate, or national endorsements like Trump support, as traders monitor these for momentum shifts.

question: Will Michael Bennet win the 2026 Colorado Governor Democratic primary election?
description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the Democratic Primary for Governor of Colorado, scheduled to take place on June 30, 2026. Resolution will be based on the overall winner of the primary, including any potential second round or run-off.

If no 2026 Colorado Gubernatorial Democratic Primary takes place, this market will resolve to “Other.”

The resolution source for this market will be the first official announcement of the results from the Colorado Democratic Party; however, an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting may suffice.

events.title: Colorado Governor Democratic Primary Winner
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the Democratic Primary for Governor of Colorado, scheduled to take place on June 30, 2026. Resolution will be based on the overall winner of the primary, including any potential second round or run-off.

If no 2026 Colorado Gubernatorial Democratic Primary takes place, this market will resolve to “Other.”

The resolution source for this market will be the first official announcement of the results from the Colorado Democratic Party; however, an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting may suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket strongly favors U.S. Senator Michael Bennet as the Democratic nominee for Colorado governor in the 2026 primary, with 81% implied probability driven by his high name recognition, incumbency advantages from his Senate role, and favorable early hypothetical polling among party voters. Attorney General Phil Weiser trails at 18%, supported by his statewide prosecutorial experience and fundraising momentum, though lacking Bennet's broader profile. Lesser contenders like state Rep. David Hughes (1.8%) and activist William Moses (1.1%) lag due to limited visibility. No major candidate announcements have shifted odds recently, but private surveys and donor signals continue reinforcing Bennet's lead ahead of formal campaign launches expected in 2025.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus in the Colorado Republican gubernatorial primary heavily favors Victor Marx at 57.5% implied probability, driven by his recent surge in grassroots fundraising and strong performance in early straw polls among party activists. Barbara Kirkmeyer holds second at 28%, bolstered by her state Senate experience and endorsements from establishment figures like former Gov. Bill Owens. Daniel Thomas at 19.1% gains from district-level wins and rural support, while Greg Lopez's 8.9% reflects prior congressional bids but faces field fragmentation. Recent catalysts include Marx's viral campaign launch event last week and Kirkmeyer's legislative scorecard release, shifting odds amid low overall polling; the June 2026 primary looms as the next key event.

This market will resolve according to the winner of the Republican Primary for Governor of Colorado, scheduled to take place on June 30, 2026. Resolution will be based on the overall winner of the primary, including any potential second round or run-off.

If no 2026 Colorado Gubernatorial Republican Primary takes place, this market will resolve to “Other.”

The resolution source for this market will be the first official announcement of the results from the Colorado Republican Party; however, an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting may suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus in the Colorado Republican gubernatorial primary heavily favors Victor Marx at 57.5% implied probability, driven by his recent surge in grassroots fundraising and strong performance in early straw polls among party activists. Barbara Kirkmeyer holds second at 28%, bolstered by her state Senate experience and endorsements from establishment figures like former Gov. Bill Owens. Daniel Thomas at 19.1% gains from district-level wins and rural support, while Greg Lopez's 8.9% reflects prior congressional bids but faces field fragmentation. Recent catalysts include Marx's viral campaign launch event last week and Kirkmeyer's legislative scorecard release, shifting odds amid low overall polling; the June 2026 primary looms as the next key event.

Baseless tabloid speculation from years-old casual encounters lacks momentum, and upcoming catalysts like Perry's potential 2025 album or Trudeau's election prospects offer no romantic upside, reinforcing market skepticism on this fringe celebrity-politico matchup by 2026.

question: Maduro exiled to Qatar by March 31?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Venezuelan President Nicolas Maduro is exiled to Qatar by March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. 

Maduro will be considered to be exiled to Qatar if he is expelled from, or voluntarily leaves, Venezuela to live in freedom in Qatar for an extended period. 

Announcements of Maduro's exile will not qualify, he must actually move there. 

Visits or trips to Qatar will not count.

Imprisonment or detention in Qatar will not count. Freedom of movement is not required for a qualifying exile, as long as Maduro is not being held as a prisoner or detainee. 

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

Confidence remains high absent a military defection or negotiated transition—both low-probability events given historical loyalty patterns—though sudden unrest or international intervention could shift dynamics before the deadline.

question: USD.AI FDV above $4B one day after launch?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Fully Diluted Valuation of USD.AI's token is greater than the value specified in the title 1 day after launch. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No."

The token must be actively, publicly transferable and tradable to be considered a launch.

The FDV will be determined using the total token supply multiplied by the token price.

"1 day after launch" is defined as 4:00 PM ET on the calendar day following launch. The resolution source for this market is the most liquid price source available. If USD.AI (https://usd.ai/) doesn't launch a token by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

question: Ukraine officially agrees to a US backed ceasefire framework by March 31?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Ukraine officially agrees to a peace framework to end the Russo–Ukrainian war that the United States has formally endorsed by March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

A qualifying peace framework is any publicly announced plan, roadmap, or framework intended as the basis for ending the war, provided that the United States formally endorses it and Ukraine officially agrees to it through one of the following:

1. A written or signed framework-related instrument issued or signed by Ukraine that affirms agreement with the U.S.-endorsed framework.

2. An official U.S.–Ukraine announcement — defined as an official government-issued declaration, such as a joint statement, communiqué, or coordinated official releases, explicitly stating that Ukraine has agreed to a U.S.-endorsed peace framework. The announcement must be issued through authorized government channels, including official written releases or formally published transcripts by the White House, State Department, Office of the President of Ukraine, Cabinet of Ministers, or Ministry of Foreign Affairs. Verbal (including interviews or remarks during bilateral meetings) and social-media claims by either leader will not qualify unless later issued in an official written or published form by the respective government. The U.S. President or any US government official publicly stating that “an agreement has been reached,” without a corresponding formal release or communiqué, will not qualify; the same applies to statements by the Ukrainian President or Ukrainian officials.

3. A formally issued Ukrainian governmental action — including a presidential decree, Cabinet resolution, or National Security and Defense Council decision — explicitly stating that Ukraine agrees to the U.S.-endorsed framework. 

The primary resolution source will be official government documents, statements, decrees, and credible reporting confirming that Ukraine officially agreed to a U.S.-endorsed peace framework under the standards above.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects low implied probabilities for Ukraine officially agreeing to a US-backed ceasefire framework soon, driven primarily by Kyiv's firm insistence on robust security guarantees and no territorial concessions amid Russian battlefield gains in Donetsk. Recent US election results and President-elect Trump's pledges for rapid peace talks have sparked optimism, including his direct outreach to Zelenskyy and Putin, yet no concrete framework has emerged from official channels. Skepticism persists due to past failed negotiations like Istanbul 2022. Key upcoming events include Trump's January 20 inauguration and potential early summits, which could catalyze movement or expose sticking points on NATO membership and sanctions relief.

question: Meloni out as Prime Minister of Italy by June 30?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Giorgia Meloni ceases to be the Prime Minister of Italy for any period of time between December 5, 2025 and June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

An announcement of Giorgia Melon's resignation/removal before this market's end date will immediately resolve this market to "Yes", regardless of when the announced resignation/removal goes into effect.

The resolution source for this market will be the government of Italy, however a consensus of credible reporting will also suffice.

events.title: Meloni out as Prime Minister of Italy by June 30?

eventMetadata.context_description: Giorgia Meloni's commanding parliamentary majority from her 2022 coalition victory underpins the strong trader consensus against her exit as Italy's Prime Minister by June 30, with no-confidence motions absent and government stability intact. Recent legislative successes, including the 2024 budget passage and EU recovery fund negotiations, have bolstered her position amid favorable polls showing Brothers of Italy leading ahead of the June 6-9 European Parliament elections. Coalition frictions with allies like Matteo Salvini's League have eased without triggering crises, while Meloni's high approval ratings—around 40%—reflect public backing for her migration and economic policies, leaving scant catalysts for an abrupt leadership change.

A mid-October Emerson College poll showed Pickens at 42% to Durand's 18%, bolstering her position amid low-turnout dynamics typical of Idaho's small Democratic electorate. No major shifts since latest FEC filings on October 15.

question: Will Maxine Durand win the 2026 Idaho Governor Democratic primary election?
description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the Democratic Primary for Governor of Idaho, scheduled to take place on May 19, 2026. Resolution will be based on the overall winner of the primary, including any potential second round or run-off.

If no 2026 Idaho Gubernatorial Democratic Primary takes place, this market will resolve to “Other.”

The resolution source for this market will be the first official announcement of the results from the Idaho Democratic Party; however, an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting may suffice.

question: Will Mexico win Group A in the 2026 FIFA World Cup?
description:
This market will resolve according to the team that wins Group A in the 2026 FIFA World Cup group stage, scheduled for June 11-27, 2026.

If multiple teams tie as group winners, this market will resolve according to the official tiebreak procedure of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If the World Cup group stage is cancelled, postponed after September 30, 2026, or there is otherwise no winner declared for this group within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from FIFA (https://www.fifa.com/); however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Mexico leads trader consensus at 47.5% implied probability to win FIFA World Cup Group A, driven by their robust qualifying form, unbeaten streak in recent CONCACAF matches, and depth in midfield with players like Luis Chávez shining. South Korea trails at 23.5%, bolstered by Son Heung-min's goal-scoring prowess and strong AFC qualifier results, though fatigue from club duties looms. The bundled 27% for Czech Republic, Denmark, North Macedonia, and Ireland reflects their inconsistent UEFA campaigns and key absences—Denmark without Christian Eriksen due to injury—limiting upside. South Africa lags at 5.5% amid CAF struggles and defensive vulnerabilities exposed in latest friendlies, underscoring Mexico's edge in momentum and squad quality shaping market pricing.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus slightly favors the BIH/ITA/NIR/WAL cluster at 45% implied probability to win FIFA World Cup Group B, edging Switzerland's 39% amid tight group-stage dynamics where no team dominates. Italy's pedigree and Bosnia's resilience counter Switzerland's consistent qualifying form and tactical discipline under coach Murat Yakin, while Northern Ireland and Wales add wildcard upset potential from recent competitive results. Canada's 15% reflects solid CONCACAF momentum but road challenges, and Qatar's 2.8% trails due to hosting distractions and weaker AFC depth. Recent friendlies and Nations League show parity in head-to-heads and goal differentials, keeping advancement odds bunched with rest advantages and defensive solidity pivotal.

eventMetadata.context_description: **Brazil's pedigree as a youth soccer powerhouse anchors its 78% implied probability to win FIFA World Cup Group C ahead of the U-17 Women's tournament opener on October 16.** The Seleção's U-17 squad boasts depth from a dominant South American qualifying run, where they topped their group unbeaten, blending technical flair with physicality that has yielded multiple youth titles. Morocco's 15% share reflects their breakout African campaign and recent senior World Cup buzz, positioning them as live underdogs with pacey attackers. Scotland (4.3%) and Haiti (1.1%) trail due to tougher qualifiers and lower rankings, though home-neutrals in the Dominican Republic add minor upset potential. No major injuries reported; traders weigh Brazil's rest edge post-qualifiers against Morocco's momentum.

question: Will Africa win the 2026 FIFA World Cup?
description:
This market will resolve to the continent of the country that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup, currently scheduled for June 11-July 19, 2026.

For example, if France wins the tournament, the market will resolve to Europe.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is cancelled, postponed after December 31, 2026, or there is otherwise no winner declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The definitive source of the continent for each country will be World Population Review (https://worldpopulationreview.com/country-rankings/list-of-countries-by-continent).

The primary resolution sources for this market will be official information from FIFA (https://www.fifa.com/) and the World Population Review; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: Which continent will win the 2026 Fifa World Cup?
events.description:
This market will resolve to the continent of the country that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup, currently scheduled for June 11-July 19, 2026.

eventMetadata.context_description: Europe's commanding 69.5% implied probability stems from its unparalleled depth of elite national teams—Spain's Euro 2024 triumph, alongside powerhouses like France, England, Germany, and Portugal—bolstered by UEFA's 16 qualification slots and players dominating top club leagues. South America's 21.5% share reflects Argentina's Copa América repeat and Brazil's talent resurgence amid ongoing CONMEBOL qualifiers, where the duo leads despite Uruguay and Colombia's form. Lower odds for Africa (4.0%), buoyed by Morocco's 2022 semifinal run but hampered by inconsistent CAF results; North America (2.4%), aided by host auto-berths yet limited by USA's Copa struggles; Asia (1.6%) and Oceania (0.4%) trail due to historical underperformance and weaker FIFA rankings, even with the expanded 48-team format. Trader consensus weighs recent continental tournament momentum and qualification progress heavily.

question: Will Albania win Eurovision 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to the country whose candidate for Eurovision 2026 wins.

If at any point it is impossible for the listed candidate to win Eurovision 2026 based on the rules of the competition (i.e. they are eliminated), this market may immediately resolve to "No".

If no winner is announced by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve "Other". All ties will be broken according to EBU's official Eurovision rules.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Eurovision (https://eurovision.tv/), including live footage of Eurovision 2026, however a consensus of credible reporting will suffice.

events.title: Eurovision Winner 2026
events.description:

eventMetadata.context_description: Finland tops Eurovision 2026 winner odds at 37.3% implied probability, propelled by strong fan buzz around potential UMK entrant Erika Vikman, whose high-energy pop style echoes recent Nordic successes like Denmark's runner-up Saba in 2024. Traders also favor Denmark and France at 12.7% each, citing Denmark's consistent televote strength and France's polished ballad tradition, bolstered by 2024 semifinal dominance. Greece edges in at 6.7% on rising Eastern Mediterranean appeal post-2024 televote surges. Early national selection teases and 2025 rehearsal hype fuel volatility, with markets eyeing host city reveals post-May 2025 Basel final as key catalysts amid secret jury-ballot unpredictability.

question: Will SpaceX's market cap be less than $500B at market close on IPO day?
description:
This market will resolve based on SpaceX's market capitalization at the closing price on its first day of trading.

If no SpaceX IPO occurs by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "No IPO before 2028".

Market capitalization expresses the monetary value of a company’s outstanding shares, stated in its pricing currency. It is calculated as the number of shares outstanding multiplied by the closing share price on the first trading day.

If the relevant value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

Resolution will be based on the primary exchange’s official listing page. In the event that the relevant figure is not displayed, another reliable source will be used.

In the event of an interruption in the course of the normal trading session on SpaceX’s first day of trading (e.g., a circuit breaker or half-day), the market will resolve according to the official closing price of the abbreviated session. If no such official closing price is published, the market will resolve according to the next trading day on which an official closing price is published, treating that as the first day of trading for purposes of this market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus overwhelmingly favors a SpaceX IPO closing above $1 trillion (90.5% implied probability), driven by the company's private valuation surging from $210 billion in mid-2024 to a potential $350 billion in ongoing tender offers, fueled by Starlink's projected $15 billion annual revenue from 7 million subscribers and Starship's accelerating test cadence toward orbital reusability. Government contracts with NASA and the DoD, plus dominance in satellite broadband, underpin this trajectory, with historical precedents like Tesla's post-IPO multiples suggesting feasible growth. Challenges include FAA regulatory delays on Starship, potential Starlink spin-off complexities, or macroeconomic headwinds delaying an IPO before 2028 (4.3% odds), which could cap valuations below trillion-dollar thresholds.

question: Rider Broncs vs. Delaware Fightin' Blue Hens
description:
In the upcoming CBB game, scheduled for December 13 at 12:00 AM ET:

If the Rider Broncs win, the market will resolve to "Rider Broncs".

If the Delaware Fightin' Blue Hens win, the market will resolve to "Delaware Fightin' Blue Hens".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.

If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.

The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods.

eventMetadata.context_description: Delaware Fightin' Blue Hens hold a commanding 73.9% implied probability as traders price in their superior recent form and matchup edge over Rider Broncs in this college basketball clash. Delaware rides a four-game win streak with balanced scoring led by guard Gerald Drumgoole Jr., averaging 78.5 points per game while holding opponents under 70. Rider, mired in a 1-5 skid, contends with frontcourt injuries to TJ Holyfield and key guard issues, exposing defensive weaknesses ranked outside the top 300 nationally. Home-court dynamics at Bob Carpenter Center further tilt sentiment, reflecting the wisdom of crowds on Delaware's momentum in non-conference play.

question: Tim Walz charged by March 31, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if any Federal or State jurisdiction of the United States formally charges or otherwise announces a criminal indictment of Governor Tim Walz by March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

For the purposes of this market the District of Columbia and any county, municipality, or other subdivision of a State shall be included within the definition of a State.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from US governmental sources, however a wide consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects low implied probability of Minnesota Governor Tim Walz facing formal criminal charges, driven primarily by unsubstantiated Republican allegations of "stolen valor" over his National Guard service record, including claims he exaggerated combat experience and retired prematurely without completing required training for Command Sergeant Major rank. No official investigations or indictments have been announced by Minnesota Attorney General Keith Ellison or federal authorities, despite GOP letters urging DOJ probes amid VP campaign scrutiny. Recent Vance-Walz debate amplified the issue without new evidence, while upcoming Election Day on November 5 may delay any partisan-driven actions, underscoring political motivations over prosecutorial merit.

question: Will Netanyahu visit NYC by March 31?
description:
If Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu visits New York City by March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Yes". Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purpose of this market, a "visit" is defined as Netanyahu physically entering the terrestrial territory of New York City, and setting foot on the ground. Whether or not Netanyahu enters New York City's airspace or maritime territory during the timeframe of this market will have no bearing on a positive resolution.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the governments of Israel, the United States, or New York City, or official information from Netanyahu or released by his verified social media accounts (e.g. https://twitter.com/netanyahu); however, a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

Netanyahu's focus remains on the Israel-Hamas war, including Gaza military operations, hostage release talks, and domestic security cabinet sessions, rendering foreign travel logistically and politically improbable amid heightened tensions. No recent catalysts—like U.N. invitations or bilateral summits—have emerged, and with the deadline days away, traders price in near-zero risk. A sudden crisis prompting an emergency U.N. address could theoretically alter this, but no such developments are evident.

question: Netanyahu arrested by March 31?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu is arrested or detained by law enforcement by March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Temporary holding at a detention facility while awaiting a judge’s decision on whether to grant a detention warrant qualifies, so long as they are held by law enforcement in an official capacity during that time.

A qualifying arrest/detention includes:
* Being taken into physical custody by law enforcement authorities (including federal, state, local, military, or international law enforcement)
* Voluntarily surrendering to law enforcement authorities in response to an arrest warrant
* Being formally booked or processed following detention
* Being handcuffed by a state authority and taken to a police station
* Surrendering at a police station or courthouse with their attorney
* Being placed under house arrest or electronic monitoring

The following scenarios will NOT qualify as an arrest/detention
* An arrest warrant being issued but not executed
* Being questioned or interviewed without arrest
* Being named in an indictment without arrest

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the arresting government and law enforcement; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors no arrest of Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu by March 31, driven by Israel's firm rejection of International Criminal Court (ICC) jurisdiction, following the November 2024 issuance of warrants against him for alleged war crimes in Gaza. As a non-ICC member state, Israel has vowed non-compliance, with no extradition precedent for its leaders, and Netanyahu retains office amid ongoing domestic corruption trials that have not led to detention. Recent Knesset actions reinforcing sovereignty further solidify this stance. While improbable, scenarios like a snap election ousting him or escalated international pressure could shift dynamics, though current stability underpins the 99.5% "No" odds reflecting crowd wisdom.

question: EU dissolves before 2027?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the European Union (EU) dissolves by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The European Union will be considered to be dissolved if any of the following conditions are met:

1) More than half of the EU member states (as of market creation) formally withdraw from the EU.

2) An official treaty or agreement is adopted between all EU member states to repeal or nullify the Treaty on European Union or the Treaty on the Functioning of the European Union.
 
3) The European Union otherwise ceases to exist as a legal entity.

EU member states will be considered to have withdrawn once they officially initiate their withdrawal and/or formally notify the European Council of their intention to withdraw, regardless of whether the withdrawal is finalized after this market’s timeframe.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the European Union and EU member states; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: EU dissolves before 2027?

2) An official treaty or agreement is adopted between all EU member states to repeal or nullify the Treaty on European Union or the Treaty on the Functioning of the European Union.
 
3) The European Union otherwise ceases to exist as a legal entity.

EU member states will be considered to have withdrawn once they officially initiate their withdrawal and/or formally notify the European Council of their intention to withdraw, regardless of whether the withdrawal is finalized after this market’s timeframe.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the European Union and EU member states; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus reflects near-certainty that the European Union will endure intact before 2027, driven by its structural resilience through past crises like Brexit, the eurozone debt turmoil, COVID-19, and Russia's invasion of Ukraine, which instead bolstered member state cohesion via joint sanctions and aid packages. No major economy has invoked Article 50 since 2016, and recent European Parliament elections saw centrist majorities prevail despite far-right gains, with Ursula von der Leyen securing re-election as Commission president. Enlargement talks with Ukraine and others signal ongoing integration, while economic interdependence and shared defense needs deter exits. Realistic risks remain limited to black-swan events like a severe sovereign debt crisis or populist referendums in France or Italy, though legal hurdles and timelines make them improbable by 2027.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors no EU member state withdrawing before 2027, reflecting the absence of viable exit movements amid entrenched economic interdependence and Brexit's cautionary economic fallout. Post-2020, no national government has pursued formal Article 50 proceedings, with Eurosceptic parties like France's National Rally abandoning "Frexit" pledges and Italy's Brothers of Italy embracing EU leadership under Meloni. Recent 2024 European Parliament elections boosted right-wing groups but preserved centrist majorities focused on reform over rupture, while Poland's pro-EU shift and Hungary's vetoes stop short of defection. Polls show exit support below 20% in key states, underscoring stability despite fiscal tensions.

question: Will Paramount close Warner Bros. acquisition by end of 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if, Paramount (directly or through a subsidiary) acquires control of Warner Bros. Discovery's studios and streaming businesses by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Resolution will be based on official company communications and regulatory filings from Paramount and Warner Bros. Discovery (or any successor entities), supplemented as needed by a consensus of reporting from major reputable news outlets.

events.title: Will Paramount close Warner Bros. acquisition by end of 2026?
events.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if, Paramount (directly or through a subsidiary) acquires control of Warner Bros. Discovery's studios and streaming businesses by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Resolution will be based on official company communications and regulatory filings from Paramount and Warner Bros. Discovery (or any successor entities), supplemented as needed by a consensus of reporting from major reputable news outlets.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket implies a 67.7% probability that Paramount will close a Warner Bros. acquisition by end-2026, fueled primarily by speculation around media sector consolidation amid streaming wars and cord-cutting pressures. No official announcements or filings confirm such a deal—recent developments instead highlight Paramount's July 2024 agreement to merge with Skydance Media in an $8B all-stock transaction, pending shareholder approval in Q4 2024 and regulatory review. Antitrust hurdles from FTC/DOJ scrutiny over overlapping film, TV, and streaming assets (Paramount+ and Max) loom large, compounded by Paramount's smaller $8B market cap versus Warner Bros. Discovery's $20B. Key catalysts include Skydance deal outcomes and potential post-merger pivots, though financial mismatches temper realism.

question: Will Netflix close Warner Bros acquisition?
description:
This market will resolve according to the first entity that acquires control of Warner Bros. Discovery's studios and streaming businesses by June 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET. 

Transactions that involve only Warner Bros. Discovery's linear television networks, news channels, or other non-studio, non-streaming assets, without also transferring control of its studios and streaming businesses, will not qualify.

Announcements of non-finalized arrangements — including, the currently announced Netflix agreement to acquire Warner Bros. Discovery’s studios and streaming businesses — will not qualify.

If no entity acquires control of Warner Bros. Discovery's studios and streaming businesses by June 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "None by June 30 2027". 

Resolution will be based on by a consensus of reporting.

events.title: Who will close Warner Bros. acquisition?
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the first entity that acquires control of Warner Bros. Discovery's studios and streaming businesses by June 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET. 

Transactions that involve only Warner Bros. Discovery's linear television networks, news channels, or other non-studio, non-streaming assets, without also transferring control of its studios and streaming businesses, will not qualify.

Announcements of non-finalized arrangements — including, the currently announced Netflix agreement to acquire Warner Bros. Discovery’s studios and streaming businesses — will not qualify.

If no entity acquires control of Warner Bros. Discovery's studios and streaming businesses by June 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "None by June 30 2027". 

Resolution will be based on by a consensus of reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket strongly favors Paramount closing a Warner Bros. acquisition at 75% implied probability, reflecting media sector consolidation pressures amid streaming losses and high debt loads for Warner Bros. Discovery (over $40 billion). Paramount's advancing Skydance merger, nearing regulatory approval after FCC and DOJ reviews, positions it as a scaled buyer capable of absorbing assets like studios and linear networks. The 16% "None by June 30, 2027" reflects antitrust hurdles seen in past deals like the blocked Penguin Random House merger. Netflix (2.5%) and Comcast (0.3%) trail due to mismatched strategic fits—Netflix shuns legacy TV, Comcast focuses on cable. Recent catalysts include WBD's Q2 earnings miss and asset sale explorations, boosting deal speculation without firm bids.

question: Will Merab Dvalishvili fight Petr Yan next?
description:
This market will resolve according to the next UFC fighter that Merab Dvalishvili is officially announced to face in a UFC bout.

Resolution for this market will be based on the next UFC fighter that Merab Dvalishvili is officially announced to fight, regardless of whether the fight ends up taking place.

Only official announcements from the UFC, which include a scheduled date for the bout, will count. Announcements with no date or which do not confirm that the fight is official, speculation, or other unofficial announcements will not count.

If Dvalishvili is officially announced to fight any non-listed fighter or no qualifying announcement is made by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the UFC (https://www.ufc.com/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Petr Yan's dominant 90.5% implied probability as Merab Dvalishvili's next opponent reflects UFC CEO Dana White's recent confirmation that the winner of Yan vs. Deiveson Figueiredo at UFC 308 on October 26 earns a bantamweight title shot, with Yan entering as a heavy -500 favorite due to his former champion pedigree, wrestling edge, and strong showings against top contenders like Song Yadong. Yan's ranking at No. 4 and recent momentum bolster trader confidence in his eliminator victory. Realistic challenges include Figueiredo's power-punching upset potential (mirroring his 7.8% odds), a potential injury withdrawal, or UFC pivoting to Umar Nurmagomedov's undefeated streak amid Khabib Nurmagomedov's advocacy, though the official path currently favors Yan.

question: Will Donald Trump visit Syria in 2026?
description:
If U.S. President Donald Trump visits a listed country between January 1 and December 31, 2026 11:59 PM ET, the relevant market will resolve to "Yes". Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purpose of this market, a "visit" is defined as Trump physically entering the terrestrial or maritime territory of the listed country. Whether or not Trump enters the country's airspace during the timeframe of this market will have no bearing on a positive resolution.

The primary resolution source for this information will be official information from government of the United States of America, official information from Trump or released by his verified social media accounts (e.g. https://twitter.com/POTUS), however, a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket for Donald Trump's 2026 international visits hinges on his first-term patterns and emerging second-term foreign policy signals, with no official itinerary announced as of early 2025. High implied probabilities favor traditional allies like Saudi Arabia—site of his 2017 debut trip—and Israel amid ongoing Middle East tensions, while Mexico and Canada reflect trade and border priorities under USMCA renewal talks. Recent cabinet picks, including foreign policy hawks like Marco Rubio for State, boost odds for confrontational destinations like China or Ukraine, but geopolitical flux from Ukraine aid debates and NATO summits could shift dynamics. Key upcoming events include the G7 summit (host TBD) and potential bilateral deals post-inauguration.

question: Tabi FDV above $500M one day after launch?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Fully Diluted Valuation of Tabi's governance token is greater than the value specified in the title 1 day after launch. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No."

The token must be actively, publicly transferable and tradable to be considered a launch.

The FDV will be determined using the total token supply multiplied by the token price.

"1 day after launch" is defined as 4:00 PM ET on the calendar day following launch. The resolution source for this market is the most liquid price source available. If Tabi (https://x.com/Tabichain) doesn't launch a token by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors Merab Dvalishvili (89.5%) as Petr Yan's next opponent, driven by reports of UFC targeting a bantamweight title rematch after Dvalishvili's unanimous decision win over Yan at UFC 288 and his recent vacant title capture from Sean O'Malley at UFC 306. Yan's ranking climb to No. 4 via decision victory over Tony Gravely in March bolsters his contention status, while limited elite options exist amid Umar Nurmagomedov's injury recovery and Deiveson Figueiredo's transition from flyweight. This implied probability reflects booking momentum for a high-stakes grudge match, though shifts could arise if Nurmagomedov heals quickly for a title queue jump or O'Malley pushes for immediate redemption, altering Yan's path.

This market will resolve according to the next UFC fighter that Petr Yan is officially announced to face in a UFC bout.

Resolution for this market will be based on the next UFC fighter that Petr Yan is officially announced to fight, regardless of whether the fight ends up taking place.

Only official announcements from the UFC, which include a scheduled date for the bout, will count. Announcements with no date or which do not confirm that the fight is official, speculation, or other unofficial announcements will not count.

If Yan is officially announced to fight any non-listed fighter or no qualifying announcement is made by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the UFC (https://www.ufc.com/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors Merab Dvalishvili (89.5%) as Petr Yan's next opponent, driven by reports of UFC targeting a bantamweight title rematch after Dvalishvili's unanimous decision win over Yan at UFC 288 and his recent vacant title capture from Sean O'Malley at UFC 306. Yan's ranking climb to No. 4 via decision victory over Tony Gravely in March bolsters his contention status, while limited elite options exist amid Umar Nurmagomedov's injury recovery and Deiveson Figueiredo's transition from flyweight. This implied probability reflects booking momentum for a high-stakes grudge match, though shifts could arise if Nurmagomedov heals quickly for a title queue jump or O'Malley pushes for immediate redemption, altering Yan's path.
